2604.24461.title: Measuring Successful Cooperation in Human-AI Teamwork: Development and Validation of the Perceived Cooperativity and Teaming Perception Scales
2604.24461.paper_summary: As human-AI cooperation becomes increasingly prevalent, reliable instruments for assessing the subjective quality of cooperative human-AI interaction are needed. We introduce two theoretically grounded scales: the Perceived Cooperativity Scale (PCS), grounded in joint activity theory, and the Teaming Perception Scale (TPS), grounded in evolutionary cooperation theory. The PCS captures an agent's perceived cooperative capability and practice within a single interaction sequence; the TPS captures the emergent sense of teaming arising from mutual contribution and support. Both scales were adapted for human-human cooperation to enable cross-agent comparisons. Across three studies (N = 409) encompassing a cooperative card game, LLM interaction, and a decision-support system, analyses of dimensionality, reliability, and validity indicated that both scales successfully differentiated between cooperation partners of varying cooperative quality and showed construct validity in line with expectations. The scales provide a basis for empirical investigation and system evaluation across a wide range of human-AI cooperation contexts.
2604.24461.paper_summary_zh: 隨著人類與人工智慧合作的普遍性日益增加，需要可靠的工具來評估合作的人類與人工智慧互動的主觀質量。我們介紹了兩個理論基礎的量表：基於共同活動理論的感知合作性量表（PCS）和基於進化合作理論的團隊感知量表（TPS）。PCS 捕捉了代理在單一互動序列中的感知合作能力和實踐；TPS 則捕捉了由於相互貢獻和支持而產生的團隊感。這兩個量表已經調整為適用於人類之間的合作，以便進行跨代理比較。在三項研究（N = 409）中，包括合作紙牌遊戲、LLM 互動和決策支持系統，對維度性、可靠性和有效性的分析表明，這兩個量表成功區分了不同合作質量的合作夥伴，並顯示出與預期一致的構念效度。這些量表為跨越各種人類與人工智慧合作背景的實證研究和系統評估提供了基礎。
2604.24461.author: Christiane Attig et.al.
2604.24461.authors: Christiane Attig, Christiane Wiebel-Herboth, Patricia Wollstadt, Tim Schrills, Mourad Zoubir, Thomas Franke
